data.title: Bitcoin Struggles to Maintain Key Moving Average, Signaling Market Correction
data.description: Matrixport's recent analysis highlights Bitcoin's inability to maintain levels above the crucial 21-week moving average, indicating the market is still in a correction phase. According to NS3.AI, despite a tactical rebound towards the end of December, Bitcoin has not succeeded in reclaiming and holding prices above this moving average. The firm observes that there are currently no definitive indicators suggesting a sustained new uptrend in the near future.

data.title: Winter Storm Fernan Disrupts Bitcoin Mining Operations in the US
data.description: The US winter storm 'Fernan' has significantly affected Foundry USA's Bitcoin mining pool, leading to a substantial decrease in its hashrate. According to NS3.AI, the hashrate has dropped by approximately 60% since Friday, resulting in around 200 exahashes per second (EH/s) going offline. This disruption has caused a slowdown in Bitcoin block production, with the average block time increasing to 12 minutes.

data.title: Asia Market Open: Bitcoin Dips Under $88K, Gold Hits Record Above $5K As Yen Hits Two-Month Peak
data.description:
Bitcoin slipped below $88,000 as Asian stocks were mixed and investors piled into gold, lifting prices to a record above $5,000 an ounce.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-price-gold-record-yen-hits-2-month-peak/

data.title: Matrixport: Bitcoin breaks key support level; market remains in correction phase.
data.description: In its latest chart analysis, Matrixport points out that Bitcoin remains in a correction phase after breaking below a key technical level. It states that the 21-week moving average is a core indicator for observing the shift between bullish and bearish trends in Bitcoin. Historically, this moving average has provided good follow-up signals during uptrends and often warns of risks before sharp pullbacks, helping investors avoid significant downward volatility. Reviewing the current price action, Matrixport notes that when Bitcoin broke below the 21-week moving average in the fourth quarter, it already indicated a potential for further market correction. Although a rebound occurred in late December, the price quickly came under pressure and fell back after approaching the 21-week moving average, failing to regain a foothold above it. Based on this assessment, Bitcoin's overall structure remains in a correction range. Matrixport also points out that a tactical rebound is possible in the short term, but so far, there are no clear signals sufficient to support a new round of sustained upward movement.

data.title: Potential Growth in Prediction Markets by 2026
data.description: Crypto KOL Sanyi.eth posted on X about the potential rise of prediction markets as a significant narrative by 2026. While platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi face uncertainties, and others seem overly competitive, Sanyi.eth suggests exploring aggregators and tools within the prediction market sector. These platforms may issue tokens more rapidly than the primary projects. Additionally, there has been notable interest from industry leaders in similar projects. Sanyi.eth plans to compile and share more detailed insights in the coming days.

data.title: Matcha Meta: The SwapNet security incident did not involve the 0x core contract; the function of directly setting limits in the aggregator has been removed.
data.description: Matcha Meta issued a statement on its X platform, noting a security incident related to SwapNet. Some users who disabled "One-Time Approval" on Matcha Meta may have been affected. The statement said they have contacted the SwapNet team, who have temporarily suspended their contracts. After reviewing with the 0x protocol team, it was confirmed that the incident did not involve 0x's AllowanceHolder or Settler core contracts. Users interacting with Matcha Meta via One-Time Approval can use the service safely. To prevent similar incidents from recurring, the feature allowing users to directly set limits for aggregators has been removed. Previously, it was reported that a security vulnerability on SwapNet resulted in the theft of approximately $16.8 million in cryptocurrency.

data.title: Matcha Meta Addresses Security Incident Linked to SwapNet
data.description:
Matcha Meta has reported a security incident related to SwapNet on the X platform. According to Odaily, users who disabled the 'One-Time Approval' feature on Matcha Meta may be affected. Matcha Meta has contacted the SwapNet team, which has temporarily suspended its contract. After reviewing with the 0x protocol team, it was confirmed that the incident does not involve the core contracts of 0x's AllowanceHolder or Settler. Users interacting with Matcha Meta through one-time approval can continue to use the service safely. To prevent similar incidents, the feature allowing users to set limits directly with the aggregator has been removed.

Previously, SwapNet experienced a security breach resulting in the theft of approximately $16.8 million in cryptocurrency.

data.title: Coinbase: Most institutional investors believe Bitcoin is undervalued in the $85,000 to $95,000 range.
data.description: In its "Charting Crypto Q1 2026 Report," Coinbase stated that approximately 70% of institutional investors believe Bitcoin is undervalued in the $85,000 to $95,000 range. The report shows that Coinbase surveyed 75 institutional investors and 73 independent investors between early December 2025 and early January 2026. Of these, 71% of institutional investors and 60% of independent investors believed Bitcoin was currently undervalued; approximately 25% of institutional investors believed it was reasonably valued, while only 4% believed it was overvalued. During the survey period, Bitcoin's price generally fluctuated within the $85,000 to $95,000 range. Coinbase pointed out that since reaching its all-time high of approximately $126,000 in October 2025, Bitcoin's price has fallen by more than 30%, significantly underperforming precious metals such as gold and silver, as well as the US stock market. The crypto market remains cautious due to geopolitical tensions and tariff uncertainties. Regarding investment expectations, 80% of institutional investors indicated they would continue holding or buy on dips if the market fell another 10%; over 60% of surveyed institutions reported maintaining or increasing their crypto asset allocations since October. Furthermore, 54% of institutions believe the market is still in an "accumulation phase" or bear market territory. Coinbase also anticipates that the Federal Reserve may implement two interest rate cuts totaling 50 basis points in 2026, potentially providing some macroeconomic support for risk assets, including crypto assets.

data.title: Coinbase: Solana blockchain integration completed and trading now open.
data.description: Coinbase announced on its X platform that it has completed the Solana blockchain integration, and users in the United States (excluding New York) and Brazil can now trade millions of Solana tokens on Coinbase.

data.description:
XRP price extended losses and traded below $1. 880. The price is now consolidating and might decline further if it remains below $1. 920
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/xrp-price-bearish-continuation-1-90/

data.title: Everstake Transfers Over 4,500 ETH to Anonymous Address
data.description: At 12:00, a significant transfer of 4,585.91 ETH was made from Everstake to an anonymous address beginning with 0xA103. According to ChainCatcher, the transaction involved a substantial amount of Ethereum, highlighting ongoing movements within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences January Decline Amid Geopolitical and Economic Factors
data.description:
Bitcoin briefly fell below $87,000 early on January 26, erasing gains made earlier in the month. According to BlockBeats, the cryptocurrency's return rate for January stands at -0.5%. On January 14, Bitcoin had surged past $97,000, marking a decline of approximately 10.9% from its monthly peak.

The downturn is attributed to several factors, including geopolitical risks, the diminished appeal of Bitcoin as a safe haven asset, the Federal Reserve's slower pace of interest rate cuts, institutional ETF outflows, and market deleveraging.

Historical data reveals that since 2013, Bitcoin's average return rate for January is +3.81%, with a median return rate of +0.62%. Over the past 13 years, Bitcoin has seen gains in January seven times and declines six times.

data.title: High-Frequency Trader Closes ETH Short Position with Loss
data.description:
On January 26, a high-frequency trader known as 'Pioneer of Short Selling' closed an ETH short position, incurring a loss of $12,500 after holding it for three hours. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the account currently shows an unrealized loss of $49,000.

The trader employs a strategy involving multiple assets with strict risk management, achieving a total profit of $2.367 million over the entire trading cycle.

data.title: Bitcoin Languishes 30% Below Peak as Gold Breaks $5,000, Highlighting Crypto's Risk-On Reality
data.description:
Precious metals surge to records while digital assets sink into "extreme fear" amid geopolitical turmoil and macro uncertainty
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2026/01/26/bitcoin-languishes-30-below-peak-as-gold-breaks-5-000-highlighting-cryptos-risk-on-reality/

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer Between Anonymous Addresses Observed
data.description: A significant Bitcoin transaction was observed involving anonymous addresses. According to ChainCatcher, at 11:51, a total of 179.98 BTC was transferred from one anonymous address to another. Following this, a portion of the Bitcoin, specifically 11 BTC, was moved to a different anonymous address. The identities behind these addresses remain unknown.

data.title: OpenMind Launches APP Builder to Facilitate Robot Software Development
data.description: OpenMind, a company specializing in intelligent machine infrastructure, has introduced APP Builder, a tool designed to simplify the creation and deployment of robot software applications. According to Foresight News, the official statement from OpenMind highlights that APP Builder aims to expand the application scenarios for autonomous machines by making it easier for developers to build and release software applications for robots.

data.title: Global Institutions Reduce Dollar Exposure as Reserve Share Declines
data.description: The share of the U.S. dollar in global foreign exchange reserves has decreased significantly. According to PANews, Cointelegraph reports that the dollar's share has fallen from 65% in 2001 to just 40% currently. This decline indicates a trend among global institutions to reduce their exposure to the dollar.

data.title: A major whale swapped WBTC back to WETH, increasing its ETH holdings by 6.45%.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale (0x455...433E) who converted its ETH holdings of four years to WBTC at the beginning of the year has recently converted its WETH back. On January 3rd, this address converted 14,145.93 WETH to 492.16 WBTC at a ratio of 0.03479, worth $44.195 million; on January 26th, it converted 578.66 WBTC back to 17,706.74 WETH at a ratio of 0.03268, worth $50.33 million. This operation increased its ETH holdings by 6.45%. Currently, this address holds 17,707 WETH and 195.49 WBTC on-chain, with a total value of $67.38 million.

data.title: BNB Chain's Growth Spurs Demand for Fixed Rate DeFi Products
data.description: Crypto KOL Gigiz.eth posted on X about the rapid expansion of meme coins and the impressive growth of BNB Chain following the Fermi upgrade. The upgrade has led to a surge in Total Value Locked (TVL) and user numbers, thanks to low gas fees and high throughput, making it attractive to retail investors and project developers. Despite the excitement, challenges remain in the DeFi sector, particularly with the prevalence of floating interest rates in lending protocols. These rates can fluctuate dramatically, causing uncertainty for both borrowers and lenders, and deterring institutional investors from committing large funds.TermMaxFi recently published an article titled 'Why BNB Chain Needs Fixed Risk and Structured Products,' highlighting key points. Firstly, BNB Chain has quietly become a leading blockchain with active users and a rich ecosystem, yet lacks advanced DeFi tools, especially fixed-rate lending options. Floating rates pose significant risks, with borrowers fearing rate spikes and lenders concerned about zero returns. Fixed rates can stabilize costs and returns, making financial planning more predictable.Structured products, such as one-click looping and leverage strategies, offer users advanced ways to earn interest and amplify returns, while supporting tokenized stock collateral. These institutional-grade strategies could elevate the ecosystem. The maturity of the ecosystem progresses in stages: the first two stages focused on user acquisition and TVL growth, and now the third stage requires fixed risk tools to attract substantial capital and truly take off.For DeFi to transition from a retail playground to mainstream finance, fixed interest rates are essential. Ethereum has seen success with platforms like Pendle and Morpho, and BNB Chain's advantages of low fees and high efficiency make it a natural fit for such developments. Institutional involvement could enhance liquidity, depth, and pricing, strengthening the BNB ecosystem further.The question remains whether fixed interest rates will become the next major narrative by 2026. Community members are encouraged to share their thoughts in the comments section.

data.title: Rieder Leads in Fed Successor Odds, Polymarket Data Shows
data.description:
Polymarket data indicates that BlackRock's Global Fixed Income Chief Investment Officer, Rieder, has a 47% implied probability of succeeding at the Federal Reserve. According to ChainCatcher, this surpasses former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's 28%, current Fed Governor Christopher Waller's 9%, and White House National Economic Advisor Kevin Hassett's 8%.

The event has seen a total trading volume exceeding $250 million. Rieder's pricing curve accelerated in January, overtaking competitor Kevin Warsh on January 23.
